Process

How It Works

01

Inquire

02

03

04

Delivered

Materials are dispatched and delivered to your site or warehouse.

About Us

Forty Years of Getting This Right

eightbyfour didn't start as a platform. It started as Sundeep Plywoods & Hardware — a plywood, MDF, particle board and hardware business founded over 40 years ago by the late Deepak Kumar Agarwal, which went on to build modular kitchens and wardrobes for homes and businesses across the city.

Four decades in the trade taught us one thing above everything else: most people don't get cheated because they made a bad decision. They get cheated because no one gave them a real one.

Every Space Deserves the Right Material

Roti, kapda, makaan — food, clothing, shelter. It's one of the most basic needs a person has, and yet the decisions behind it are some of the least transparent. Which grade of ply. Whether MDF or HDHMR is right for this room. Whether what shows up on site is actually what was ordered.

Shelter isn't only the home you return to at night. It's the office you build a business in, the store you open to customers, the space a family or a company puts down roots in. eightbyfour exists for all of it — residential and commercial, built from the ground up or renovated from the inside out.

We Guide. Then We Deliver.

eightbyfour isn't a material shop. It's a guide that gets you everything you need to build the right space.

We help you choose the right material for what you're actually building — not what's easiest to move off the shelf. And because we don't stop at procurement, we handle the execution too: modular kitchens, wardrobes, and the interior work that turns raw material into a finished space.

The material you chose is the material that gets installed. No swaps, no surprises, no one to blame but us if it goes wrong — which is exactly how we like it.

The Standard Hasn't Moved in 40 Years

Sell people what's right for them, not what's easiest for you. That's what built Sundeep Plywoods & Hardware, and it's what eightbyfour is built on today — for anyone who wants a partner in the material, not just a supplier of it.

Reference

Material & Grade Notes

What does MR grade mean?

MR stands for Moisture Resistant. It suits normal interior use — cabinets, wardrobes, and furniture in dry areas — but isn't built for standing water or continuous humidity.

What does BWP grade mean?

BWP stands for Boiling Water Proof, the highest bonding standard for plywood. It's tested by boiling and is typically used in kitchens, bathrooms, and other high-moisture areas.

What's the difference between BWP and BWR?

BWR (Boil Water Resistant) sits between MR and BWP — it tolerates occasional moisture exposure but isn't rated for the constant water contact that BWP handles.

What does FR grade mean?

FR stands for Fire Retardant — treated to slow the spread of flame. It's common in commercial interiors, public buildings, and areas with stricter fire-safety requirements.

What's the standard plywood sheet size?

8×4 ft (96×48 in) is the industry-standard sheet size, stocked as the default by most manufacturers. Other sizes are typically available on request.

Which thickness should I choose?

As a general guide: thinner sheets (4–6mm) suit backs and shutters, mid-range (9–12mm) suits general cabinetry, and 18–19mm+ suits load-bearing panels like shelving. Confirm exact requirements with your fabricator, or ask us via Inquire.

What is HPL (High Pressure Laminate)?

HPL is a decorative sheet made of resin-impregnated layers fused under high pressure and heat, then bonded onto plywood, MDF, or particle board. It's the standard surface finish for cabinet doors, wardrobes, and countertops.

What's a matching edge band for?

An edge band is a thin strip applied to the exposed edge of a panel to hide the raw core material and give it a finished look. Ordering it color-matched to the laminate sheet keeps the visible edge and face consistent.
